title: Receptive Field Grower
tagline: Stack convolutions, watch the reach of a deep feature spread
summary: Convolutions are local — a single 3×3 kernel only sees 9 pixels. But stack them, and the receptive field balloons. Drag the depth slider, flip between same-kernel and mixed-kernel stacks, and see exactly which input pixels influence a deep feature. The VGG 'stack 3×3 convs' insight becomes obvious.
highlights: Start with one 3×3 conv — RF = 3 pixels wide.
Stack three 3×3 convs — RF grows to 7×7, but with fewer params than a single 7×7.
Compare stride 1 vs stride 2 vs dilation — same depth, very different RF growth.
See parameter count computed live: 3×(3·3·C²) = 27C² vs 1×(7·7·C²) = 49C².
Preview the effective receptive field — the practical center-weighted Gaussian shape.
